[2018-06-29 22:11] _sakuya (Sakuya): I always did wonder how you could do "upgrades" in prismata
[2018-06-29 22:11] elyot: in the past we also had downgrades
[2018-06-29 22:11] elyot: like a shitty cheap unit you could upgrade to a splitter
[2018-06-29 22:11] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I actually really like leviathan. The only thing is it feels a little 'boring' but it definitely has some good use cases
[2018-06-29 22:12] _sakuya (Sakuya): Yea leviathan seems fun
[2018-06-29 22:12] elyot: we might end up shipping the grimbotch version or something
[2018-06-29 22:13] miuna6933 (Aka): i like the ideas for all of them except big pikachu
[2018-06-29 22:13] miuna6933 (Aka): i dunno how to fix them though
[2018-06-29 22:13] elyot: big pikachu is fucking great, you buy it and then buy engineers the next turn and it's so awkward to manage energy
[2018-06-29 22:14] miuna6933 (Aka): it just feels too similar to vore and caut as you're aware
[2018-06-29 22:14] .jrkirby (jrkirby): seems unique enough to me
[2018-06-29 22:15] .jrkirby (jrkirby): the stamina makes it stand out, from a play-feel standpoint

[2018-06-29 22:15] .jrkirby (jrkirby): it'd probably be good with eggs

[2018-06-29 22:19] _sakuya (Sakuya): And how it breaks attack potential
[2018-06-29 22:19] _sakuya (Sakuya): Because theres both apollo and deadeye which seem fairly similar

[2018-06-29 22:20] .jrkirby (jrkirby): and they didn't want to do that work over again
[2018-06-29 22:20] Deleted User (Deleted User): apple is a one of and deadeye doesn't apply
[2018-06-29 22:20] elyot: it's simpler than apollo but yes it requires explicit programming
[2018-06-29 22:21] miuna6933 (Aka): even if just adding a * it would need an if statement
[2018-06-29 22:21] miuna6933 (Aka): i guess you could make it accurate but that requires effort
[2018-06-29 22:21] miuna6933 (Aka): max(opponent klystrons, own enginners)

It only creates the wall if it desn't delete the tarsier eh and it doesn't delete the tarsier if you continue to try to delete the tarsier

[2018-06-30 03:57] f300xen (f0x): therefore inflation or something
[2018-06-30 03:57] wilson0862 (Wilson): That's what I mean though. Mahar saying restore without a max HP is only potentially ambiguous imo *because* of the other units.

[2018-06-30 03:58] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): well sure, but I was talking about the best way if there is no threat
[2018-06-30 03:58] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): obviously it's op against threat

[2018-06-30 03:58] f300xen (f0x): obviously they need to start at max health but construct gauss charges for opponent

[2018-07-02 13:38] supergreyman (Greyman): First, git gud. Then we'll talk. :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-07-02 13:39] supergreyman (Greyman): I've played with it only once so far. So sad...
[2018-07-02 13:41] swapgo: Double G is a big commitment in any set that isn't big on G
[2018-07-02 13:41] sorawotobu: innervi is strong but i don't think it's too strong. it's also the kind of unit you wouldn't mind being too strong anyway since a powerful soak unit is less likely to cause autowin-ish games than powerful tech buildings for example
[2018-07-02 13:42] swapgo: And F is still probably better in situations where you want to squeak just a few more points of constant attack
[2018-07-02 13:42] sorawotobu: and i think 5GG innervi would be pretty weak and less interesting
[2018-07-02 13:44] sorawotobu: 4GG innervi is just fine to buy as soak even if it's not playing around threat. 5GG innervi would only be good against threat and even then it would be a lot worse than other options
[2018-07-02 13:44] supergreyman (Greyman): If there's no other good green units, would you get 2 Conduits for it?
[2018-07-02 13:45] sorawotobu: i guess sometimes i would? sounds a little bad
[2018-07-02 13:46] supergreyman (Greyman): Based on my experience, it's pretty good against exhaust attackers like Wincer and Scorchilla.
[2018-07-02 13:46] swapgo: You can't get too greedy though
[2018-07-02 13:46] swapgo: or they just will wait it out
[2018-07-02 13:47] swapgo: You have to buy it one turn before the shot, if you buy it two turns before they'll just not attack and exploit you probably
[2018-07-02 13:47] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I make sure to have like 4HP innervi by the time they're ready to attack.
[2018-07-02 13:47] supergreyman (Greyman): So they would have to wait out two turns.
[2018-07-02 13:48] supergreyman (Greyman): Basically non-prompt Infusion Grid.
[2018-07-02 13:48] swapgo: Yeah, if they wait two turns to 'exploit' it when you do that they just lost out on a lot of attack anyways, so you end up winning
[2018-07-02 13:49] supergreyman (Greyman): I think Innervi is in a pretty good spot overall.
[2018-07-02 13:50] supergreyman (Greyman): Good but not too good.
[2018-07-02 13:51] swapgo: If your opponent buys Innervi that you know won't die on defense, and you have Apollo
[2018-07-02 13:51] swapgo: Do you shoot it or do you shoot down a S?
[2018-07-02 13:52] supergreyman (Greyman): Would you kill Steelsplitter or Infusion Grid? I would kill Steelsplitter.
[2018-07-02 13:52] supergreyman (Greyman): Expert may say otherwise.
[2018-07-02 13:53] swapgo: Yeah that's usually what I'm doing
[2018-07-02 13:53] swapgo: For some reason it doesn't *feels* like it's helping though, but I think it does
[2018-07-02 13:54] supergreyman (Greyman): I evaluate it based on who's more expensive.
[2018-07-02 13:54] supergreyman (Greyman): If he keeps replacing Steelsplitter every turn, he'll lose more money than replacing Innervi.
[2018-07-02 13:56] supergreyman (Greyman): Attacks are also half turn quicker, thus the payout for taking out Steelsplitter is quicker.
[2018-07-02 13:57] swapgo: Against threat it's a marginally cheaper IGrid
[2018-07-02 13:57] swapgo: Mind you, cheaper strictly in the sense of G is worth 1.3
[2018-07-02 13:58] supergreyman (Greyman): So I guess this is an indirect nerf to Drake.
[2018-07-02 13:58] swapgo: Sure it makes things like Drake worse because they have a counterplay
[2018-07-02 13:58] swapgo: Yeah most threat units can simply crack their threat to get rid of it
[2018-07-02 13:58] swapgo: like Nade Mechs and whatnot
[2018-07-02 13:58] swapgo: Drake is the one where doing so is super expensive
